pattern: Two prefix sums leave a divisible gap exactly when they share a remainder mod k, so counting how many prefixes fall in each remainder bucket turns the problem into pair-counting.
hints: A subarray sum equals the difference of two prefix sums; it is divisible by k when those prefixes are congruent modulo k.
Group prefix sums by their remainder mod k; any two in the same group bound a valid subarray.

Maintain a running prefix sum and a count of seen remainders (start with remainder 0 counted once for the empty prefix). For each new remainder, add the count already stored before incrementing it. Normalize negatives with ((p % k) + k) % k.

edge_inputs.description: mix of positives, zero, and negatives
single element that is itself divisible
zeros each form a divisible subarray
the whole array sums to a multiple but no proper prefix does

stress.description: random signed values with a small modulus so many congruences collide
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(-30, 30) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(2, 9)
    return [nums, k]

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def subarraysDivByK(self, nums, k):
        from collections import defaultdict
        seen = defaultdict(int)
        seen[0] = 1
        prefix = 0
        count = 0
        for x in nums:
            prefix = (prefix + x) % k
            r = prefix % k
            count += seen[r]
            seen[r] += 1
        return count
